title: Pacific Fleet announces USS Carl Vinson CSG conducting integrated operations with Japan and Australia in Philippine Sea
summary: US Pacific Fleet announced that the USS Carl Vinson Carrier Strike Group commenced integrated maritime operations with Japan Maritime Self-Defense Force and Royal Australian Navy units in the Philippine Sea, demonstrating trilateral interoperability.
contents:
# USS Carl Vinson CSG Conducts Trilateral Operations in Philippine Sea

## Operational Announcement
US Pacific Fleet announced commencement of integrated maritime operations involving the USS Carl Vinson Carrier Strike Group alongside Japan Maritime Self-Defense Force and Royal Australian Navy units.

## Force Composition
- **USS Carl Vinson** (CVN-70) with Carrier Air Wing 2
- **USS Princeton** (CG-59) - Ticonderoga-class cruiser
- **USS Kidd** (DDG-100) - Arleigh Burke-class destroyer
- **JS Izumo** (DDH-183) - Izumo-class helicopter destroyer (JMSDF)
- **JS Teruzuki** (DD-116) - Akizuki-class destroyer (JMSDF)
- **HMAS Brisbane** (DDG-41) - Hobart-class destroyer (RAN)

## Exercise Activities
- Anti-submarine warfare exercises
- Integrated air defense operations
- Surface warfare tactical maneuvering
- Cross-deck helicopter operations
- Communication interoperability drills

## Location
Operations conducted in the Philippine Sea, east of the Philippines and south of Japan.

## Strategic Context
PACFLT Commander stated these operations demonstrate the strength and depth of trilateral cooperation and commitment to a free and open Indo-Pacific. The exercise follows recent PLA military activities in the region and reinforces allied presence.
